data.title: Chewy expected to post small Q3 beat; Foot Locker saw softer demand in October: Piper
Simon Property portfolio traffic rises 6.4% on Black Friday weekend
data.description:
Traffic at Simon Property Group's (NYSE:SPG) shopping centers in the U.S. climbed 6.4% Y/Y, amid preliminary reports of surging sales from retailers, the company said on Monday.

The increase was even more pronounced at malls, where traffic jumped 7.1%.

"Popular brands throughout our portfolio reported double-digit sales increases over the weekend compared to last year," said CEO and President David Simon. "Coast to coast, we saw lines prior to opening and throughout the day at many of our centers across the country."

For Black Friday, alone, traffic increased 5.9% Y/Y, then accelerated to 6.3% on Saturday and 8.2% on Sunday.

Nevertheless, Simon Property (NYSE:SPG) stock dipped 1.1% in early afternoon trading on Monday.

data.title: U.S. is said to ready $725M arms package for Ukraine
data.description:
• The United States is preparing to announce the latest arms package for Ukraine drawn from U.S. inventories, Bloomberg News reported Monday, citing officials with knowledge of the matter.
  • The value of the package is about $725 million, and includes a second shipment of antipersonnel mines that President Joe Biden authorized.
  • The 71st Presidential Drawdown Authority package may be announced as soon as Monday. It is the largest since April, when the administration issued military aid valued at $1 billion.
  • Other the battery-powered anti-personnel mines, which can be set to deactivate over a certain timeframe, the latest package has antitank, counter-drone and other munitions, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: Coinbase integrates Apple Pay to Onramp for fiat-to-crypto purchases
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) added Apple Pay (NASDAQ:AAPL) for all fiat-to-crypto purchases to its Onramp product for onboarding to crypto, as it seeks to streamline what can be a cumbersome process, the company announced on Monday.

The tool eases fiat-to-crypto conversions with "lightweight" know-your-customer ("KYC") requirements for eligible purchases, the company said.

"Onboarding to crypto can be a pain for your users: it can take a long time, require them to go through lengthy KYC (often requiring back and forth interactions), and use multiple apps," Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) said in a blog post.

"With Apple Pay, getting onchain only takes seconds," it said.

Apps that already use Coinbase Onramp already have access to the feature. For the app users, Apple Pay will automatically appear as an option when making an eligible purchase.

Coinbase (COIN) stock jumped 3.8% in early afternoon trading on Monday. That comes on a day when bitcoin (BTC-USD) slipped 2.1% to $95.3K.

data.title: Fossil stock skyrockets as CEO ups stake by 250K shares
data.description:
• Shares of Fossil Group (NASDAQ:FOSL) were launched more than 55% higher on Monday in very heavy volume as CEO Franco Fogliato disclosed a substantial purchase of the company’s stock.
  • In a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday, Fogliato bought 250K shares in two separate transactions, raising his stake in Fossil (FOSL) to 1.750M shares.
  • Fogliato was appointed CEO in September.
  • As a result of the transaction, FOSL rallied to a 4-month high of $2.61.

data.title: Super Micro jumps as internal review finds no misconduct; to replace CFO
data.description:
Update (12:45 PM ET): Adds latest share movement

Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) extended intraday gains to ~31% on Monday after an independent special committee found no evidence of misconduct by the board of directors or audit committee.

No restatement of reported financials is expected based on the findings of the review. Super Micro’s balance sheet came under the scanner after its auditor resigned citing concerns over the company's governance, transparency, and internal control measures.

The artificial intelligence server maker has since appointed BDO USA as its new independent auditor and submitted a plan with the Nasdaq to achieve compliance and avoid getting delisted.

The independent review found no evidence to support the statements made by Ernst & Young in its resignation letter as Super Micro’s (NASDAQ:SMCI) auditor.

The investigation took over three months and did find some lapses in processes. As part of the investigating committee’s recommendations, Super Micro will appoint a new chief financial officer, chief compliance officer and general counsel, and has named Kenneth Cheung, previously the vice president of finance, as its new chief accounting officer.

Super Micro (SMCI) expects to become current with its financial reports within the discretionary period as part of its compliance plan to Nasdaq. It does not expect any restatements of its quarterly reports for the fiscal year 2024 or for prior fiscal years.

data.title: Argentina government approves proposed TGS Vaca Muerta gas pipeline project
data.description:
Argentina's government said Monday it approved a $500M natural gas pipeline expansion project in the Vaca Muerta shale, in a key signal of state support to increase transport capacity as it ramps up domestic energy production.

The project proposed by local operator Transportadora de Gas del Sur (NYSE:TGS) would expand transport capacity from the huge Vaca Muerta shale region to Buenos Aires province.

The project is a rare attempt by Argentina's private sector to take the lead expanding its gas pipeline network, as new libertarian President Milei tries to entice more private investment to overturn an energy deficit and export more gas.

Transportadora de Gas del Sur (TGS) originally presented the plan in June and said its proposal, which is subject to it winning the tender, would install six compressor tube sets at four plants, increasing the amount of gas the existing pipeline could carry.

The project would allow the company to add 14M cm/day of gas transportation capacity to the 21M cm/day it already transports, to reach 35M cm/day along the entire route.

data.title: Kymera upped to overweight by Wells Fargo on upcoming data
data.description:
• Wells Fargo has upgraded Kymera Therapeutics (NASDAQ:KYMR) to overweight from equal weight, citing upcoming clinical data for its drug candidate KT-621.
  • The investment bank said Kymera (KYMR) shares could rise 75% on a Phase 1 update for KT-621, a STAT6 degrader, in the first half of 2025.
  • “Even with year-to-date performance, the Street’s assigned value for KT-621 is still fairly conservative and Wells is buyers at these levels,” the bank said.
  • The bank added it believes the data will provide “a clear rationale for STAT6’s role in TH2-driven diseases.”
  • Wells Fargo set its price target for the stock at $57.

data.title: Olema in deal with Novartis on Kisqali breast cancer combo collaboration
data.description:
• Olema Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:OLMA) will collaborate with Novartis (NYSE:NVS) on a clinical trial examining a combination of the former's palazestrant in combination with the latter's Kisqali (ribociclib) for first-line ER+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer.
  • The agreement includes the Swiss drugmaker providing Kisqali for the upcoming phase 3 OPERA-02 trial that will enroll ~1000 participants. That trial is expected to begin in mid-2025.
  • Olema also said it has received $250M in private placement equity. Those funds will allow the biopharma to conduct OPERA-02, a phase 1/2 study of OP-3136, a candidate for breast, prostate and lung cancers, and the ongoing phase 3 OPERA-01 trial, which is examining palazestrant as monotherapy.
  • Topline data from OPERA-01 is expected in 2026 and new data from an ongoing phase 1b/2 study of palazestrant + Kisqali will be presented at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium later this month.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher acquires Hann Insurance Brokers
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) announced on Monday the acquisition of Lambton, New South Wales-based Hann Insurance Brokers.
  • The terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release
